title: Create a governed network design packet
persona: Supply chain strategy lead with operations edit access
steps: Review the live baseline, recommended scenario, service risks, cost risks, missing-data gaps, scenario tradeoffs, approval plan, and rollback plan.
expected: A persisted AssistantNetworkDesignPacket is created with baselineJson, scenarioJson, tradeoffJson, serviceImpactJson, riskExposureJson, approvalPlanJson, rollbackPlanJson, and leadershipSummary.
The packet recommends one scenario from baseline_no_change, rebalance_constrained_facilities, consolidate_fragmented_lanes, or dual_source_supplier_regions.
No facility, lane, supplier, customer, order, shipment, inventory, RFQ, tariff, booking, or carrier allocation is mutated automatically.

title: Queue network design review for approval
persona: Operations controller with WMS or Control Tower edit access
steps: Create or select a DRAFT network design packet.
Add a review note describing the intended footprint decision.
expected: The packet status changes to REVIEW_QUEUED with actionQueueItemId, approvedByUserId, approvedAt, and approvalNote.
An AssistantActionQueueItem is created with actionKind assistant_network_design_review and objectHref /assistant/network-design.
An AssistantAuditEvent states that network master data and downstream operational records were not changed automatically.

title: Missing network data and guardrails remain visible
steps: Open AMP33 with incomplete facility country, lane origin/destination, or supplier country data in the tenant.
Review the live preview and create a packet.
Inspect the approval and rollback sections.
expected: The packet baselineJson includes missingDataCount and the score reflects data-quality gaps.
approvalPlanJson requires validation with facility, lane, customer, and supplier owners before downstream work.
rollbackPlanJson keeps current facility, lane, supplier, customer, order, inventory, shipment, RFQ, tariff, and carrier allocation records unchanged until separate approval.
